step_by_step_reasoning: We identify the nodes directly from the whiteboard icons: the users/audience, AWS WAF, Application Load Balancer (ALB), ECS Fargate (represented as a single icon), MemoryDB for Redis, VPN, On-Premises Editorial System, CloudWatch, Kinesis Data Firehose, and Amazon S3. The workflow flows from the users through security and load balancing to Fargate and MemoryDB. Admin/editorial commands flow from the on-premise systems via VPN to Fargate and MemoryDB. Log data is sent to CloudWatch, then streamed via Firehose into S3.

graph.name: Mediaset Real-Time Voting Architecture
graph.notes: Serverless, low-latency architecture designed to process >5 million votes within 10ms per vote under highly spiky traffic conditions.

nodes.name: Audience / Voting Channels
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Voting via SMS, mobile apps, websites, and connected TVs.

nodes.name: AWS WAF
nodes.notes: Restricts voting to users inside Italy using geographic checks.

nodes.name: Application Load Balancer
nodes.notes: Handles incoming spiky traffic and routes to frontend containers.

nodes.name: AWS Fargate
nodes.notes: WORKLOAD_PEEK: Auto-scaled frontend and backend microservices that handle user auth, session verification, and request processing.

nodes.name: Amazon MemoryDB for Redis
nodes.notes: DATA_PEEK: Houses active contestants, active sessions, and limits. Executes Lua scripts for atomic vote counting under 10ms.

nodes.name: AWS Site-to-Site VPN
nodes.notes: Securely tunnels admin/editorial commands from on-premises network.

nodes.name: On-Premises Editorial System
nodes.notes: Used by editorial teams to manage sessions and read final voting results for the broadcast.

nodes.name: Amazon CloudWatch
nodes.notes: Aggregates logs and metrics from ALB and Fargate container environments.

nodes.name: Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose
nodes.notes: Buffers and streams logs from CloudWatch for persistent archival.

nodes.name: Amazon S3
nodes.notes: Acts as the final long-term storage data lake for vote audit certification.

edges.notes: Session configuration or results command transmitted.
Saves session configuration or closes the voting session.
Forwards final results back to VPN.
Delivers results to on-premise system for host announcements.
Valid geo-checked traffic passed to ALB.
ALB distributes requests to active Fargate containers.
Executes Lua scripts in Redis to validate limits and increment counter.
Acks the vote status and updates limits remaining.
Persists log batches for compliance auditing.
